hypothesis: Peer-relative cash return-on-assets ranked over a full year captures persistent quality. Annual fundamental rank provides strong IS/OOS Sharpe with naturally low turnover from quarterly data.

Volume-confirmed sector-relative momentum: 63-day return momentum multiplied by relative volume rank. The two signals reinforce each other and the combination produces strong Sharpe in the 20-30% turnover range — acceptable because the signal is robust.

Profitability improvement blended with analyst estimate revision within sector. Two independent signals (fundamental + sentiment) produce higher Sharpe than either alone. Moderate turnover (~20%) is fine given the signal strength.

Statistical return overreaction (Z-score extreme) combined with quality anchor within sector. The quality filter prevents buying genuinely bad companies; the reversal signal provides the Sharpe edge. Turnover ~15-25%.
